excerpt: Next-gen container runtime with zero-copy DAG storage and P2P image sync. Run OCI images as Linux containers, Firecracker microVMs, or Apple Silicon VMs. - pullrun/pullrun
content_html:
⚡ 30-Second Install • 🚀 Quick Start • 🏗️ Architecture • 🎯 Why Pullrun? • 🗺️ Feature Map • ☸️ Kubernetes • 🤖 AI Agents

⚡ What is Pullrun?

Pullrun runs the same OCI image as a container or a VM. It stores layers in a content-addressed DAG (no overlayfs), syncs blocks peer-to-peer, and ships as a ~14 MB CLI + ~6 MB runtime daemon.

Why this matters: Modern infrastructure uses too many execution engines — Docker for dev, containerd for production, Firecracker for isolation, CRI for Kubernetes, MCP agents for AI. Each has its own image format, storage, and operational model — even though they all run the same OCI images. Pullrun collapses these layers into one runtime.

Key differentiators:

  • Containers and VMs from the same image — no separate VM build step, no separate VM image format
  • Content-addressed DAG store — zero-copy mmap reads, deduplicated by content hash, byte-identical across every node
  • P2P image distribution — one registry pull per cluster, rest sync peer-to-peer at LAN speed
  • ~14 MB CLI + ~6 MB runtime daemon (stripped) — no daemon required by default (CLI-only pullrun run), optional daemon for background services

Also included: Kubernetes CRI shim (beta), Docker Compose support, MCP server for AI agents, policy engine (Cosign, SBOM, seccomp), P2P sync layer, and AES-256-GCM encrypted secrets — all in the same binary.

# Apple Silicon VM (macOS default) — 3 s
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun" --attach -t

# Firecracker microVM (Linux) — 400 ms
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --backend vm --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun" --attach -t

# Container (Linux) — 400 ms
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

# Windows WSL2 — same image, same command, same store
pullrun.exe run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

📦 Install

# One command, any platform
curl -fsSL https://github.com/pullrun/pullrun/raw/main/install.sh | bash
Platform What you get
macOS brew tap pullrun/tap && brew install pullrun → native binary, no Xcode
Linux APT package or direct download, systemd service (requires runc for containers, /dev/kvm for VMs)
Windows pullrun.exe + WSL2 auto-provisioning, runc + Firecracker
Manual install options 
# macOS (Homebrew — pre-built, no build deps)
brew tap pullrun/tap && brew install pullrun

# From source
make build && export PATH="$PWD/bin:$PATH"

# Cross-compile Windows CLI
cd cli/pullrun && GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o pullrun.exe .

🚀 Quick Start

# ── Pull any OCI image ──────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun pull alpine:3.18     # 968 ms — benchmark script at hack/bench.sh

# ── Run as a container (Linux) ─────────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

# ── Run as a microVM (macOS/Linux) ─────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --backend vm --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello" --attach -t

# ── Interactive shell with detach ──────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --tty --attach --cmd /bin/sh
#   Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q  → detach (workload keeps running)
#   pullrun exec <id> -t /bin/sh → re-attach

# ── Background workload ────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd /bin/sleep --cmd 3600

# ── Build & push (no Docker daemon needed) ─────────────────────
pullrun build ./Dockerfile . -t myapp:latest
pullrun build ./Dockerfile . -t myapp:latest --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64
pullrun push <root-digest> ghcr.io/myorg/myimg:latest

# ── Export/import for air-gapped ───────────────────────────────
pullrun save <digest> -o myimage.tar
pullrun load -i myimage.tar

# ── Compose (separate binary) ────────────────────────────────
pullrun-compose up -f myapp/compose.yml               # containers (default on Linux)
pullrun-compose up -f myapp/compose.yml --backend vm   # same compose, VM isolation
pullrun-compose logs -f
pullrun-compose down

# ── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun list                  # all workloads (pending/running/exited)
pullrun stop <id>
pullrun exec <id> /bin/echo hello
# Re-attach to a detached workload (allocates a fresh PTY)
pullrun exec <id> -t -- /bin/sh
# Or, for bidirectional I/O streaming without a new shell:
pullrun workload run <id>

# ── Image management ──────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun images                 # list pulled images in local DAG store
pullrun rmi alpine:3.18       # remove an image, cascade-delete unreachable layers
pullrun rmi sha256:abc...     # also works by digest

# ── GC (DAG store garbage collection) ─────────────────────────
pullrun gc                    # dry-run — report what would be deleted
pullrun gc --apply            # actually delete unreachable nodes
pullrun gc --apply --force    # bypass 90% safety guard

# ── Events & Stats ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun events --types=WORKLOAD_STARTED,POLICY_DENIED
pullrun stats <id>

# ── Network ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun network create my-net --subnet 10.43.1.0/24
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --net bridge

# ── Secrets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun secret create db_password -                     # from stdin
pullrun run myapp:latest --secret db_password

# ── Configs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun config create nginx.conf ./nginx.conf       # from file
pullrun config create nginx.conf -                   # from stdin
pullrun run nginx:latest --config nginx.conf

# ── Diff & Inspect ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun diff <id>
pullrun inspect <id>
pullrun commit <id> myapp:snapshot

# ── Login ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun login ghcr.io
pullrun logout

# ── MCP (AI agents) ────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun mcp                   # stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code)
pullrun mcp --sse :8080       # SSE mode (for remote agents)

# ── P2P sync ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500

🎯 Why Pullrun?

🏗️ Architecture is Everything

Docker's overlayfs store is a filesystem overlay — CVEs in overlayfs (CVE-2023-0386, CVE-2023-32629) can let a container escape to the host. Pullrun's content-addressed DAG store stores layers as-is, verified by content hash. No overlayfs, no escape.

Pullrun also mitigates shared-kernel cross-container risks that aren't specific to any storage driver — for example, kernel page-cache bugs like CVE-2026-31431 ("Copy Fail") affect all containers sharing a host kernel, regardless of the storage backend. Per-VM kernel isolation (Firecracker, Apple Virtualization) fully contains these. The DAG store makes the VM path zero-cost: the same image, no separate VM build step.

Pullrun is also rootless by default — no sudo needed, no daemon listening on a TCP socket, no attack surface from a central dockerd.

🔥 The Only Runtime with Containers + VMs from the Same Image

Backend Isolation Pullrun Docker Podman
Linux containers (runc) Process ✅ ✅ ✅
Firecracker microVMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌
Apple Silicon VMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌
Windows WSL2 containers Process ✅ ✅ ❌
Windows Firecracker VMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌

Same image, any isolation level. No separate VM image build step. The OCI manifest IS the VM rootfs.

🧬 Content-Addressed DAG Store

Pullrun's store is built on rkyv + mmap. OCI layers are stored once, deduplicated by content hash, and mmap()'d directly — no tar extraction, no overlayfs, no dockerd process owning the data.

A sha256: digest is globally consistent. Every node that has pulled alpine:3.18 stores byte-identical files on disk. This makes P2P sync trivial: blocks are verified by content hash, transferred delta-only, and deduplicated across the entire cluster.

📊 By the Numbers

Metric Pullrun Docker
First alpine:3.18 pull 968 ms ~2 s
Container run latency ~400 ms ~800 ms
Apple Virt VM boot ~160 ms N/A
Firecracker VM cold boot ~500 ms N/A
Firecracker VM warm pool ~200 ms N/A
Idle daemon RSS 24.6 MiB ~90 MiB
Binary size (stripped) ~20 MB (CLI + runtime) ~75 MB
Rootless by default ✅ ❌ (dockerd as root)
Central daemon Optional Required

Benchmarks: single-node, cold cache, alpine:3.18 on Apple M3 (macOS 14) for Pullrun vs Docker Desktop 4.27. Container run latency measured from run command exit to workload PID alive. Apple VM boot measured to interactive exec prompt. Firecracker measurements on Linux x86_64 with KVM; warm pool configured with --vm-warm-pool-size 4. Pullrun daemon RSS measured after pullrun pull alpine:3.18 + pullrun run alpine:3.18 --tty --attach --cmd /bin/sh then detached. Docker RSS from docker run -d --name idle alpine:3.18 sleep 3600.

Benchmarked with hyperfine (≥10 iterations, mean ± stddev reported). The benchmark script lives at hack/bench.sh — run it yourself to reproduce the numbers.

✨ Features

🏃 Workload Lifecycle

pull, run, stop, exec, attach, list, logs, stats, events, inspect, prune, rmi, gc plus:

  • commit — create a new image layer from a running container's current filesystem. The DAG store computes the delta against the original image, producing a content-addressed layer.
  • diff — show file-level changes between a running workload and its original image. The store compares DAG trees to produce an add/modify/delete listing.
  • update — live resource limit changes (CPU millicores, memory bytes) without restarting the workload.
  • cp — bidirectional file copy between host and workload paths.
  • save / load — OCI-compatible single-file tarball export/import for air-gapped environments. Re-import produces identical content hashes.
  • login / logout — bearer token auth for private OCI registries.

🔧 Build

Native Dockerfile builder (FROM, RUN, COPY, ADD, WORKDIR, ENV, CMD, ENTRYPOINT) with content-addressed layer caching by instruction hash. Uses runc directly for RUN — no Docker daemon. Multi-platform builds via --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64.

🏗️ OCI Kernel Images

Kernel binaries are first-class OCI content. pullrun kernel install downloads a vmlinux from an OCI registry into the DAG store. The --kernel-image flag lets any workload specify a custom kernel by OCI reference — same store-backed, content-addressed pipeline: verified by digest, cached forever, pushable to any registry.

🐳 Compose

Full Docker Compose-compatible workflow: up, down, logs, ps, build. Supports dependency ordering (topological sort), port mapping, environment variables, volumes (bind mounts), resource limits (CPU/memory), labels, and per-project bridge networks for isolation. Parses standard docker-compose.yml files via the compose-spec/compose-go library.

Each service can run as a container or VM — use --backend vm to boot all services as Firecracker microVMs from the same compose file, no changes required.

☸️ Kubernetes CRI

Drop-in CRI shim at cri/pullrun-cri/ — implement RuntimeService and ImageService from the Kubernetes CRI API. Maps pod sandboxes to pullrun workloads, supports RuntimeClass (pullrun-container / pullrun-vm), pod annotations for image/CPU/memory overrides, and streaming (exec, attach, port-forward).

A native control-plane stub lives in control-plane/: pullrun-controller (scheduler) + pullrun-agent (per-node deployer) communicating over gRPC. This is a v1 work-in-progress with etcd, .pullrun.local DNS, and admission control planned — but the wire protocol is stable today.

🤖 MCP AI Integration

Native Model Context Protocol server exposing 15 runtime operations as MCP tools — run, stop, exec, list, get, inspect, logs, stats, pull_image, list_images, build, push, prune, compose_up, compose_down plus MCP resources (pullrun://workload/{id}, pullrun://workload/{id}/logs, pullrun://store/info, pullrun://images). Works in stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code, Cursor) or SSE mode (for remote agents via HTTP).

🔐 Policy Engine

Gate workloads before they run — built-in support for:

  • Cosign signature verification (Ed25519 key pairs, key ID matching)
  • SBOM evaluation (CVSS scoring, ban by license)
  • Seccomp profiles (default allowlist of ~50 syscalls, unconfined, or custom JSON)
  • Read-only rootfs — prevents runtime tampering (write only to --volume mounts)
  • no_new_privileges — blocks setuid and capset escalation
  • Policy is declarative: required_signature: true, max_cvss_score: 7.0, deny_licenses: ["GPL-3.0"]

Compose all four: --require-signature --readonly-rootfs --no-new-privileges --seccomp-profile default.

🌐 P2P Image Distribution

Nodes share image blocks peer-to-peer via gRPC + Bloom filters. One node pulls from the registry, the rest delta-sync from each other. Features: mDNS/discovery for zero-config LAN peer finding, Bloom filter cache to avoid redundant transfers, gossip protocol for peer state, delta computation, registrar service for peer tracking.

📡 Networking

User-defined bridge networks with IPAM, inbound/outbound port forwarding, DNS resolution, and iptables integration. Four modes per workload via --net: isolated (default for containers, loopback only with host proxy on 10.42.0.1), bridge (shared pullrun-br0 bridge, inter-workload communication), slirp (default for VMs, userspace NAT via slirp4netns — no bridge, no iptables), host (shares host namespace), none (no network). Isolation is enforced by the proxy, not per-workload VLANs.

🗝️ Encrypted Secrets

AES-256-GCM encryption at rest, decrypted into workload tmpfs at runtime. pullrun secret create/get/ls/inspect/rm — data stays encrypted on disk, only the runtime process can decrypt.

🔄 Export/Import

Single-file OCI-compatible tarball export for air-gapped environments. Re-import produces identical content hashes.

📊 Events & Observability

Real-time event stream via pullrun events — IMAGE_PULLED, WORKLOAD_STARTED, POLICY_DENIED, etc. Per-workload stats with CPU/memory. Prometheus metrics exporter built into the daemon. PrometheusRule alerting config in deploy/.

🧹 DAG Store Garbage Collection & Reference-Counted rmi

pullrun rmi removes an image by tag or digest with immediate cascade deletion of unreachable subtree nodes. Per-node refcounts (node.refcount sidecar files) preserve shared layers — a layer referenced by another image is never deleted until the last referencing image is removed. Crash recovery via recompute_all_refcounts on daemon startup rebuilds refcounts from all tagged image and workload roots.

pullrun gc reclaims unreachable DAG nodes (orphaned layers, manifests, blobs) that are no longer reachable from any tagged image or running workload. Features: dry-run by default (report only), --apply to actually delete, --force to bypass the 90% safety guard, op-lock protection for in-flight operations, VM kernel image pinning via kernel_image_digest. The store no longer grows forever.

🖥️ Interactive Shells

Full TTY support with detach/re-attach via Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q. Works across all backends (container, Firecracker VM, Apple VM). Detached workloads keep running — re-attach with pullrun exec --tty <id> /bin/sh. Even works on exited workloads (daemon starts a fresh sleep container).

❤️ Health Checks & Restart Policies

pullrun run --health-cmd 'curl -f http://localhost:80' — periodic health checks with configurable interval, timeout, and retries. --restart on-failure|always|unless-stopped controls automatic restart on exit. Health state is surfaced via pullrun inspect and pullrun events.

🗄️ VM State Persistence

Stopped VMs behave like hibernated machines — all writes preserved, restart on demand with exec. Backend specifics: Apple Virt rootfs persists via VirtioFS, Firecracker retains the ext4 image, Container (runc) rootfs is ephemeral (only --volume mounts survive). No re-pull needed.

🔐 Private Registries

pullrun login <registry> for bearer token auth. Daemon accepts --insecure-registry for plain-HTTP mirrors and air-gapped LAN caches.

☸ Kubernetes

Pullrun ships a CRI shim in cri/pullrun-cri/ that implements the Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface. It maps pod sandboxes to pullrun workloads and supports:

  • RuntimeClass — pullrun-container for runc containers, pullrun-vm for Firecracker VMs
  • Pod annotations: pullrun.io/image, pullrun.io/cpu-millicores, pullrun.io/memory-bytes
  • Streaming: exec, attach, port-forward
  • Image management via the DAG store

Deploy as a DaemonSet with manifests in deploy/:

kubectl apply -f deploy/runtime-daemon.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/serviceaccount.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/servicemonitor.yaml

🤖 MCP AI Integration

Any MCP-compatible AI agent (opencode, Claude Code, Cursor) can control pullrun through natural language:

# Start the MCP server
pullrun mcp

# In opencode or Claude Code, the agent can now:
#   "pull alpine and run it as a VM"
#   "exec into my-app and check the logs"
#   "show me all running workloads"
#   "run docker-compose up from my project"

The MCP server exposes 15 tools and 4 resource types. SSE mode for remote agents: pullrun mcp --sse :8080.

🌐 P2P Image Distribution

# Node A — seed
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500

# Node B — pulls delta blocks from Node A
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9501 \
  --sync-peers node-a.example.com:9500

Each block verified by content hash before acceptance — no trust required.

🔧 Backend Comparison

Backend Isolation Platform Boot Time Best For
🐧 Linux Containers (runc) Process (namespace) Linux, macOS, Windows (WSL2) ~400 ms Dev, CI/CD, dense packing
🔥 Firecracker microVM Per-VM kernel (KVM) Linux x86_64, Windows (WSL2 x86_64) ~500 ms cold / ~200 ms warm pool Multi-tenant, untrusted workloads, compliance, fast-recycling sandboxes
🍎 Apple Virtualization Per-VM kernel (Hypervisor.framework) macOS Apple Silicon ~160 ms macOS dev, Apple Silicon CI

🏗️ Architecture

                              pullrun (Go CLI)
                   pull · run · build · compose · inspect
                   events · stats · network · secret · mcp
                              │
                              │ gRPC (UDS or TCP)
                              ▼
                   ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │         pullrun-runtime             │
                    │  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────────────┐ │
                    │  │ Store    │   │ Executor         │ │
                    │  │ (DAG)    │   │ runc / VM        │ │
                    │  │ └ rkyv   │   │ └ VmPool (warm)  │ │
                    │  │ └ mmap   │   │                  │ │
                    │  │ └ DashMap│   │                  │ │
                   │  └────┬─────┘   └──────┬───────┘    │
                   │       │                │            │
                   │  ┌────┴────────────────┴────┐       │
                   │  │       Network            │       │
                   │  │  IPAM · Proxy · DNS      │       │
                   │  └──────────────────────────┘       │
                   │                                      │
                   │  Sync · Policy · Secrets · Metrics   │
                   └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                   ┌──────────┴──────────┐
                   │      Kubernetes     │
                   │  CRI shim · Runtime │
                   │  Class · Prometheus │
                   └─────────────────────┘

The store uses DashMap<Digest, Arc<Mmap>> for lock-free concurrent reads — the first reader pays for mmap() + page faults, every subsequent reader is a single atomic load. The Rust workspace is eleven crates (store, oci, exec, vm, net, dns, vsock, sync, policy, runtime, init); the Go side is the CLI, CRI shim, compose, and control-plane stub.

📁 Project Layout

proto/            # Protobuf definitions (single source of truth)
runtime/          # Rust workspace — core data plane
  pullrun-store/   # Zero-copy DAG store (rkyv + mmap + DashMap concurrent cache)
  pullrun-oci/     # OCI client + DAG converter
  pullrun-exec/    # Executor trait + runc wrapper
  pullrun-vm/      # Firecracker + Apple Virt backends, warm VM pool (pool.rs), pullrun-init guest agent
  pullrun-net/     # IPAM, proxy, DNS, iptables, slirp4netns
  pullrun-dns/     # In-process DNS server for workload resolution
  pullrun-vsock/   # Vsock transport layer for VM guest-host communication
  pullrun-sync/    # P2P block sync (Bloom, mDNS, gossip)
  pullrun-policy/  # Cosign, SBOM, seccomp gates
  pullrun-runtime/ # gRPC daemon
cli/pullrun/      # Go CLI (cobra) — 30+ commands
cri/pullrun-cri/  # Kubernetes CRI shim
control-plane/    # Multi-node orchestration stub: pullrun-controller + pullrun-agent (v1 WIP)
cmd/pullrun-compose/ # Docker Compose-compatible CLI
deploy/           # K8s manifests, Prometheus rules, alerts
docs/             # Architecture, operations, policy, MCP
tools/            # Standalone smoke-test workspaces (apple-virt-exec, firecracker-smoke, etc.)

📚 Documentation

Document What You'll Find
docs/PULLRUN_GUIDE.md Full user guide for all platforms
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md DAG store design, executor trait, network model
docs/OPERATIONS.md Deploying, monitoring, troubleshooting
docs/POLICY.md Policy engine (cosign, SBOM, CVSS, license)
docs/WINDOWS.md Windows/WSL2 setup, Firecracker, known issues
docs/ALL_MCP.md MCP server reference (AI agent integration)
docs/cross-node-dag-sync.md P2P block sync design

📄 Technical Report

A technical report describing the architecture is available in /paper and archived on Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20679669

── Login ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── pullrun login ghcr.io pullrun logout # ── MCP (AI agents) ──────────────────────────────────────────── pullrun mcp # stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code) pullrun mcp --sse :8080 # SSE mode (for remote agents) # ── P2P sync ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500 🎯 Why Pullrun? 🏗️ Architecture is Everything Docker's overlayfs store is a filesystem overlay — CVEs in overlayfs (CVE-2023-0386, CVE-2023-32629) can let a container escape to the host. Pullrun's content-addressed DAG store stores layers as-is, verified by content hash. No overlayfs, no escape. Pullrun also mitigates shared-kernel cross-container risks that aren't specific to any storage driver — for example, kernel page-cache bugs like CVE-2026-31431 ("Copy Fail") affect all containers sharing a host kernel, regardless of the storage backend. Per-VM kernel isolation (Firecracker, Apple Virtualization) fully contains these. The DAG store makes the VM path zero-cost: the same image, no separate VM build step. Pullrun is also rootless by default — no sudo needed, no daemon listening on a TCP socket, no attack surface from a central dockerd. 🔥 The Only Runtime with Containers + VMs from the Same Image Backend Isolation Pullrun Docker Podman Linux containers (runc) Process ✅ ✅ ✅ Firecracker microVMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌ Apple Silicon VMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌ Windows WSL2 containers Process ✅ ✅ ❌ Windows Firecracker VMs Per-VM kernel ✅ ❌ ❌ Same image, any isolation level. No separate VM image build step. The OCI manifest IS the VM rootfs. 🧬 Content-Addressed DAG Store Pullrun's store is built on rkyv + mmap. OCI layers are stored once, deduplicated by content hash, and mmap()'d directly — no tar extraction, no overlayfs, no dockerd process owning the data. A sha256: digest is globally consistent. Every node that has pulled alpine:3.18 stores byte-identical files on disk. This makes P2P sync trivial: blocks are verified by content hash, transferred delta-only, and deduplicated across the entire cluster. 📊 By the Numbers Metric Pullrun Docker First alpine:3.18 pull 968 ms ~2 s Container run latency ~400 ms ~800 ms Apple Virt VM boot ~160 ms N/A Firecracker VM cold boot ~500 ms N/A Firecracker VM warm pool ~200 ms N/A Idle daemon RSS 24.6 MiB ~90 MiB Binary size (stripped) ~20 MB (CLI + runtime) ~75 MB Rootless by default ✅ ❌ (dockerd as root) Central daemon Optional Required Benchmarks: single-node, cold cache, alpine:3.18 on Apple M3 (macOS 14) for Pullrun vs Docker Desktop 4.27. Container run latency measured from run command exit to workload PID alive. Apple VM boot measured to interactive exec prompt. Firecracker measurements on Linux x86_64 with KVM; warm pool configured with --vm-warm-pool-size 4. Pullrun daemon RSS measured after pullrun pull alpine:3.18 + pullrun run alpine:3.18 --tty --attach --cmd /bin/sh then detached. Docker RSS from docker run -d --name idle alpine:3.18 sleep 3600. Benchmarked with hyperfine (≥10 iterations, mean ± stddev reported). The benchmark script lives at hack/bench.sh — run it yourself to reproduce the numbers. ✨ Features 🏃 Workload Lifecycle pull, run, stop, exec, attach, list, logs, stats, events, inspect, prune, rmi, gc plus: commit — create a new image layer from a running container's current filesystem. The DAG store computes the delta against the original image, producing a content-addressed layer. diff — show file-level changes between a running workload and its original image. The store compares DAG trees to produce an add/modify/delete listing. update — live resource limit changes (CPU millicores, memory bytes) without restarting the workload. cp — bidirectional file copy between host and workload paths. save / load — OCI-compatible single-file tarball export/import for air-gapped environments. Re-import produces identical content hashes. login / logout — bearer token auth for private OCI registries. 🔧 Build Native Dockerfile builder (FROM, RUN, COPY, ADD, WORKDIR, ENV, CMD, ENTRYPOINT) with content-addressed layer caching by instruction hash. Uses runc directly for RUN — no Docker daemon. Multi-platform builds via --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64. 🏗️ OCI Kernel Images Kernel binaries are first-class OCI content. pullrun kernel install downloads a vmlinux from an OCI registry into the DAG store. The --kernel-image flag lets any workload specify a custom kernel by OCI reference — same store-backed, content-addressed pipeline: verified by digest, cached forever, pushable to any registry. 🐳 Compose Full Docker Compose-compatible workflow: up, down, logs, ps, build. Supports dependency ordering (topological sort), port mapping, environment variables, volumes (bind mounts), resource limits (CPU/memory), labels, and per-project bridge networks for isolation. Parses standard docker-compose.yml files via the compose-spec/compose-go library. Each service can run as a container or VM — use --backend vm to boot all services as Firecracker microVMs from the same compose file, no changes required. ☸️ Kubernetes CRI Drop-in CRI shim at cri/pullrun-cri/ — implement RuntimeService and ImageService from the Kubernetes CRI API. Maps pod sandboxes to pullrun workloads, supports RuntimeClass (pullrun-container / pullrun-vm), pod annotations for image/CPU/memory overrides, and streaming (exec, attach, port-forward). A native control-plane stub lives in control-plane/: pullrun-controller (scheduler) + pullrun-agent (per-node deployer) communicating over gRPC. This is a v1 work-in-progress with etcd, .pullrun.local DNS, and admission control planned — but the wire protocol is stable today. 🤖 MCP AI Integration Native Model Context Protocol server exposing 15 runtime operations as MCP tools — run, stop, exec, list, get, inspect, logs, stats, pull_image, list_images, build, push, prune, compose_up, compose_down plus MCP resources (pullrun://workload/{id}, pullrun://workload/{id}/logs, pullrun://store/info, pullrun://images). Works in stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code, Cursor) or SSE mode (for remote agents via HTTP). 🔐 Policy Engine Gate workloads before they run — built-in support for: Cosign signature verification (Ed25519 key pairs, key ID matching) SBOM evaluation (CVSS scoring, ban by license) Seccomp profiles (default allowlist of ~50 syscalls, unconfined, or custom JSON) Read-only rootfs — prevents runtime tampering (write only to --volume mounts) no_new_privileges — blocks setuid and capset escalation Policy is declarative: required_signature: true, max_cvss_score: 7.0, deny_licenses: ["GPL-3.0"] Compose all four: --require-signature --readonly-rootfs --no-new-privileges --seccomp-profile default. 🌐 P2P Image Distribution Nodes share image blocks peer-to-peer via gRPC + Bloom filters. One node pulls from the registry, the rest delta-sync from each other. Features: mDNS/discovery for zero-config LAN peer finding, Bloom filter cache to avoid redundant transfers, gossip protocol for peer state, delta computation, registrar service for peer tracking. 📡 Networking User-defined bridge networks with IPAM, inbound/outbound port forwarding, DNS resolution, and iptables integration. Four modes per workload via --net: isolated (default for containers, loopback only with host proxy on 10.42.0.1), bridge (shared pullrun-br0 bridge, inter-workload communication), slirp (default for VMs, userspace NAT via slirp4netns — no bridge, no iptables), host (shares host namespace), none (no network). Isolation is enforced by the proxy, not per-workload VLANs. 🗝️ Encrypted Secrets AES-256-GCM encryption at rest, decrypted into workload tmpfs at runtime. pullrun secret create/get/ls/inspect/rm — data stays encrypted on disk, only the runtime process can decrypt. 🔄 Export/Import Single-file OCI-compatible tarball export for air-gapped environments. Re-import produces identical content hashes. 📊 Events & Observability Real-time event stream via pullrun events — IMAGE_PULLED, WORKLOAD_STARTED, POLICY_DENIED, etc. Per-workload stats with CPU/memory. Prometheus metrics exporter built into the daemon. PrometheusRule alerting config in deploy/. 🧹 DAG Store Garbage Collection & Reference-Counted rmi pullrun rmi removes an image by tag or digest with immediate cascade deletion of unreachable subtree nodes. Per-node refcounts (node.refcount sidecar files) preserve shared layers — a layer referenced by another image is never deleted until the last referencing image is removed. Crash recovery via recompute_all_refcounts on daemon startup rebuilds refcounts from all tagged image and workload roots. pullrun gc reclaims unreachable DAG nodes (orphaned layers, manifests, blobs) that are no longer reachable from any tagged image or running workload. Features: dry-run by default (report only), --apply to actually delete, --force to bypass the 90% safety guard, op-lock protection for in-flight operations, VM kernel image pinning via kernel_image_digest. The store no longer grows forever. 🖥️ Interactive Shells Full TTY support with detach/re-attach via Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q. Works across all backends (container, Firecracker VM, Apple VM). Detached workloads keep running — re-attach with pullrun exec --tty <id> /bin/sh. Even works on exited workloads (daemon starts a fresh sleep container). ❤️ Health Checks & Restart Policies pullrun run --health-cmd 'curl -f http://localhost:80' — periodic health checks with configurable interval, timeout, and retries. --restart on-failure|always|unless-stopped controls automatic restart on exit. Health state is surfaced via pullrun inspect and pullrun events. 🗄️ VM State Persistence Stopped VMs behave like hibernated machines — all writes preserved, restart on demand with exec. Backend specifics: Apple Virt rootfs persists via VirtioFS, Firecracker retains the ext4 image, Container (runc) rootfs is ephemeral (only --volume mounts survive). No re-pull needed. 🔐 Private Registries pullrun login <registry> for bearer token auth. Daemon accepts --insecure-registry for plain-HTTP mirrors and air-gapped LAN caches. ☸ Kubernetes Pullrun ships a CRI shim in cri/pullrun-cri/ that implements the Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface. It maps pod sandboxes to pullrun workloads and supports: RuntimeClass — pullrun-container for runc containers, pullrun-vm for Firecracker VMs Pod annotations: pullrun.io/image, pullrun.io/cpu-millicores, pullrun.io/memory-bytes Streaming: exec, attach, port-forward Image management via the DAG store Deploy as a DaemonSet with manifests in deploy/: kubectl apply -f deploy/runtime-daemon.yaml kubectl apply -f deploy/serviceaccount.yaml kubectl apply -f deploy/servicemonitor.yaml 🤖 MCP AI Integration Any MCP-compatible AI agent (opencode, Claude Code, Cursor) can control pullrun through natural language: # Start the MCP server pullrun mcp # In opencode or Claude Code, the agent can now: # "pull alpine and run it as a VM" # "exec into my-app and check the logs" # "show me all running workloads" # "run docker-compose up from my project" The MCP server exposes 15 tools and 4 resource types. SSE mode for remote agents: pullrun mcp --sse :8080. 🌐 P2P Image Distribution # Node A — seed pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500 # Node B — pulls delta blocks from Node A pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9501 \ --sync-peers node-a.example.com:9500 Each block verified by content hash before acceptance — no trust required. 🔧 Backend Comparison Backend Isolation Platform Boot Time Best For 🐧 Linux Containers (runc) Process (namespace) Linux, macOS, Windows (WSL2) ~400 ms Dev, CI/CD, dense packing 🔥 Firecracker microVM Per-VM kernel (KVM) Linux x86_64, Windows (WSL2 x86_64) ~500 ms cold / ~200 ms warm pool Multi-tenant, untrusted workloads, compliance, fast-recycling sandboxes 🍎 Apple Virtualization Per-VM kernel (Hypervisor.framework) macOS Apple Silicon ~160 ms macOS dev, Apple Silicon CI 🏗️ Architecture pullrun (Go CLI) pull · run · build · compose · inspect events · stats · network · secret · mcp │ │ gRPC (UDS or TCP) ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ pullrun-runtime │ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ Store │ │ Executor │ │ │ │ (DAG) │ │ runc / VM │ │ │ │ └ rkyv │ │ └ VmPool (warm) │ │ │ │ └ mmap │ │ │ │ │ │ └ DashMap│ │ │ │ │ └────┬─────┘ └──────┬───────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌────┴────────────────┴────┐ │ │ │ Network │ │ │ │ IPAM · Proxy · DNS │ │ │ └──────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ Sync · Policy · Secrets · Metrics │ └──────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ┌──────────┴──────────┐ │ Kubernetes │ │ CRI shim · Runtime │ │ Class · Prometheus │ └─────────────────────┘ The store uses DashMap<Digest, Arc<Mmap>> for lock-free concurrent reads — the first reader pays for mmap() + page faults, every subsequent reader is a single atomic load. The Rust workspace is eleven crates (store, oci, exec, vm, net, dns, vsock, sync, policy, runtime, init); the Go side is the CLI, CRI shim, compose, and control-plane stub. 📁 Project Layout proto/ # Protobuf definitions (single source of truth) runtime/ # Rust workspace — core data plane pullrun-store/ # Zero-copy DAG store (rkyv + mmap + DashMap concurrent cache) pullrun-oci/ # OCI client + DAG converter pullrun-exec/ # Executor trait + runc wrapper pullrun-vm/ # Firecracker + Apple Virt backends, warm VM pool (pool.rs), pullrun-init guest agent pullrun-net/ # IPAM, proxy, DNS, iptables, slirp4netns pullrun-dns/ # In-process DNS server for workload resolution pullrun-vsock/ # Vsock transport layer for VM guest-host communication pullrun-sync/ # P2P block sync (Bloom, mDNS, gossip) pullrun-policy/ # Cosign, SBOM, seccomp gates pullrun-runtime/ # gRPC daemon cli/pullrun/ # Go CLI (cobra) — 30+ commands cri/pullrun-cri/ # Kubernetes CRI shim control-plane/ # Multi-node orchestration stub: pullrun-controller + pullrun-agent (v1 WIP) cmd/pullrun-compose/ # Docker Compose-compatible CLI deploy/ # K8s manifests, Prometheus rules, alerts docs/ # Architecture, operations, policy, MCP tools/ # Standalone smoke-test workspaces (apple-virt-exec, firecracker-smoke, etc.) 📚 Documentation Document What You'll Find docs/PULLRUN_GUIDE.md Full user guide for all platforms docs/ARCHITECTURE.md DAG store design, executor trait, network model docs/OPERATIONS.md Deploying, monitoring, troubleshooting docs/POLICY.md Policy engine (cosign, SBOM, CVSS, license) docs/WINDOWS.md Windows/WSL2 setup, Firecracker, known issues docs/ALL_MCP.md MCP server reference (AI agent integration) docs/cross-node-dag-sync.md P2P block sync design 📄 Technical Report A technical report describing the architecture is available in /paper and archived on Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20679669 📄 License Apache 2.0 — see LICENSE. Contributions subject to CLA.md. Built with Rust 🦀 and Go 🐹

sentences.zh: ⚡ 什么是 Pullrun？

blocks.html_en: Pullrun runs the same OCI image as a container or a VM. It stores layers in a content-addressed DAG (no overlayfs), syncs blocks peer-to-peer, and ships as a ~14 MB CLI + ~6 MB runtime daemon.

sentences.en: Pullrun runs the same OCI image as a container or a VM.
sentences.zh: Pullrun 运行与容器或虚拟机相同的 OCI 映像。

sentences.en: It stores layers in a content-addressed DAG (no overlayfs), syncs blocks peer-to-peer, and ships as a ~14 MB CLI + ~6 MB runtime daemon.
sentences.zh: 它将层存储在内容寻址的 DAG（无 Overlayfs）中，点对点同步块，并作为约 14 MB CLI + 约 6 MB 运行时守护程序提供。

blocks.html_en: Why this matters: Modern infrastructure uses too many execution engines — Docker for dev, containerd for production, Firecracker for isolation, CRI for Kubernetes, MCP agents for AI. Each has its own image format, storage, and operational model — even though they all run the same OCI images. Pullrun collapses these layers into one runtime.

sentences.en: Pullrun collapses these layers into one runtime.

sentences.en: Every node that has pulled alpine:3.18 stores byte-identical files on disk.
sentences.zh: 每个拉取 alpine:3.18 的节点都会在磁盘上存储字节相同的文件。

sentences.en: This makes P2P sync trivial: blocks are verified by content hash, transferred delta-only, and deduplicated across the entire cluster.
sentences.zh: 这使得 P2P 同步变得微不足道：块通过内容哈希进行验证，仅进行增量传输，并在整个集群中进行重复数据删除。

blocks.html_en: • commit — create a new image layer from a running container's current filesystem. The DAG store computes the delta against the original image, producing a content-addressed layer.

sentences.en: commit — create a new image layer from a running container's current filesystem.

sentences.en: The DAG store computes the delta against the original image, producing a content-addressed layer.
sentences.zh: DAG 存储根据原始图像计算增量，生成内容寻址层。

blocks.html_en: • diff — show file-level changes between a running workload and its original image. The store compares DAG trees to produce an add/modify/delete listing.

sentences.en: diff — show file-level changes between a running workload and its original image.
sentences.zh: diff — 显示正在运行的工作负载与其原始映像之间的文件级更改。

Supports dependency ordering (topological sort), port mapping, environment variables, volumes (bind mounts), resource limits (CPU/memory), labels, and per-project bridge networks for isolation. Parses standard docker-compose.yml files via the compose-spec/compose-go library.

blocks.html_en: Drop-in CRI shim at cri/pullrun-cri/ — implement RuntimeService and ImageService from the Kubernetes CRI API. Maps pod sandboxes to pullrun workloads, supports RuntimeClass (pullrun-container / pullrun-vm), pod annotations for image/CPU/memory overrides, and streaming (exec, attach, port-forward).

Works in stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code, Cursor) or SSE mode (for remote agents via HTTP).

sentences.zh: no_new_privileges — 阻止 setuid 和 capset 升级

sentences.zh: 🌐 P2P 图片分发

blocks.html_en: Nodes share image blocks peer-to-peer via gRPC + Bloom filters. One node pulls from the registry, the rest delta-sync from each other. Features: mDNS/discovery for zero-config LAN peer finding, Bloom filter cache to avoid redundant transfers, gossip protocol for peer state, delta computation, registrar service for peer tracking.

sentences.en: Nodes share image blocks peer-to-peer via gRPC + Bloom filters.
sentences.zh: 节点通过 gRPC + Bloom 过滤器点对点共享图像块。

sentences.en: One node pulls from the registry, the rest delta-sync from each other.
sentences.zh: 一个节点从注册表中提取数据，其余节点相互进行增量同步。

sentences.en: Features: mDNS/discovery for zero-config LAN peer finding, Bloom filter cache to avoid redundant transfers, gossip protocol for peer state, delta computation, registrar service for peer tracking.
sentences.zh: 功能：用于零配置 LAN 对等点查找的 mDNS/发现、用于避免冗余传输的布隆过滤器缓存、用于对等点状态的八卦协议、增量计算、用于对等点跟踪的注册服务。

sentences.en: 📡 Networking
sentences.zh: 📡 网络

blocks.html_en: User-defined bridge networks with IPAM, inbound/outbound port forwarding, DNS resolution, and iptables integration. Four modes per workload via --net: isolated (default for containers, loopback only with host proxy on 10.42.0.1), bridge (shared pullrun-br0 bridge, inter-workload communication), slirp (default for VMs, userspace NAT via slirp4netns — no bridge, no iptables), host (shares host namespace), none (no network). Isolation is enforced by the proxy, not per-workload VLANs.

sentences.en: User-defined bridge networks with IPAM, inbound/outbound port forwarding, DNS resolution, and iptables integration.
sentences.zh: 用户定义的桥接网络，具有 IPAM、入站/出站端口转发、DNS 解析和 iptables 集成。

sentences.en: Four modes per workload via --net: isolated (default for containers, loopback only with host proxy on 10.42.0.1), bridge (shared pullrun-br0 bridge, inter-workload communication), slirp (default for VMs, userspace NAT via slirp4netns — no bridge, no iptables), host (shares host namespace), none (no network).
sentences.zh: 每个工作负载通过 --net 有四种模式：隔离（容器默认，仅在 10.42.0.1 上使用主机代理环回）、桥接（共享 pullrun-br0 桥接、工作负载间通信）、slirp（虚拟机默认，通过 slirp4netns 进行用户空间 NAT — 无桥接、无 iptables）、主机（共享主机命名空间）、无（无网络）。

sentences.en: Isolation is enforced by the proxy, not per-workload VLANs.
sentences.zh: 隔离是由代理而不是按工作负载 VLAN 强制执行的。

blocks.html_en: AES-256-GCM encryption at rest, decrypted into workload tmpfs at runtime. pullrun secret create/get/ls/inspect/rm — data stays encrypted on disk, only the runtime process can decrypt.

sentences.zh: 静态 AES-256-GCM 加密，运行时解密到工作负载 tmpfs 中。

sentences.zh: pullrun Secret create/get/ls/inspect/rm — 数据在磁盘上保持加密状态，只有运行时进程才能解密。

sentences.en: Real-time event stream via pullrun events — IMAGE_PULLED, WORKLOAD_STARTED, POLICY_DENIED, etc. Per-workload stats with CPU/memory.
sentences.zh: 通过 pullrun 事件的实时事件流 — IMAGE_PULLED、WORKLOAD_STARTED、POLICY_DENIED 等。每个工作负载的 CPU/内存统计信息。

blocks.html_en: 🧹 DAG Store Garbage Collection & Reference-Counted rmi

sentences.en: 🧹 DAG Store Garbage Collection & Reference-Counted rmi
sentences.zh: 🧹 DAG 存储垃圾收集和引用计数 rmi

blocks.html_en: pullrun rmi removes an image by tag or digest with immediate cascade deletion of unreachable subtree nodes. Per-node refcounts (node.refcount sidecar files) preserve shared layers — a layer referenced by another image is never deleted until the last referencing image is removed. Crash recovery via recompute_all_refcounts on daemon startup rebuilds refcounts from all tagged image and workload roots.

sentences.en: pullrun rmi removes an image by tag or digest with immediate cascade deletion of unreachable subtree nodes.
sentences.zh: pullrun rmi 通过标签或摘要删除图像，并立即级联删除不可达的子树节点。

sentences.en: Per-node refcounts (node.refcount sidecar files) preserve shared layers — a layer referenced by another image is never deleted until the last referencing image is removed.
sentences.zh: 每个节点的引用计数（node.refcount sidecar 文件）保留共享层——在删除最后一个引用图像之前，另一个图像引用的层永远不会被删除。

sentences.en: Crash recovery via recompute_all_refcounts on daemon startup rebuilds refcounts from all tagged image and workload roots.
sentences.zh: 守护进程启动时通过 recompute_all_refcounts 进行崩溃恢复，从所有标记的映像和工作负载根重建引用计数。

blocks.html_en: pullrun gc reclaims unreachable DAG nodes (orphaned layers, manifests, blobs) that are no longer reachable from any tagged image or running workload. Features: dry-run by default (report only), --apply to actually delete, --force to bypass the 90% safety guard, op-lock protection for in-flight operations, VM kernel image pinning via kernel_image_digest. The store no longer grows forever.

sentences.en: pullrun gc reclaims unreachable DAG nodes (orphaned layers, manifests, blobs) that are no longer reachable from any tagged image or running workload.

content_html_zh:
⚡ 30 秒安装 • 🚀 快速入门 • 🏗️ 架构 • 🎯 为什么选择 Pullrun？ • 🗺️ 功能图 • ☸️ Kubernetes • 🤖 AI 代理

⚡ 什么是 Pullrun？

Pullrun 运行与容器或虚拟机相同的 OCI 映像。 它将层存储在内容寻址的 DAG（无 Overlayfs）中，点对点同步块，并作为约 14 MB CLI + 约 6 MB 运行时守护程序提供。

为什么这很重要：现代基础设施使用了太多的执行引擎——用于开发的 Docker、用于生产的 containerd、用于隔离的 Firecracker、用于 Kubernetes 的 CRI、用于 AI 的 MCP 代理。 每个都有自己的映像格式、存储和操作模型 - 尽管它们都运行相同的 OCI 映像。 Pullrun 将这些层折叠成一个运行时。

主要区别：

  • 来自同一镜像的容器和虚拟机——没有单独的虚拟机构建步骤，没有单独的虚拟机镜像格式
  • 内容寻址 DAG 存储 — 零拷贝 mmap 读取、通过内容哈希进行重复数据删除、每个节点的字节相同
  • P2P 镜像分发 — 每个集群一个注册表拉取，以 LAN 速度进行点对点同步
  • ~14 MB CLI + ~6 MB 运行时守护进程（已删除） — 默认情况下不需要守护进程（仅 CLI pullrun 运行），后台服务可选守护进程

还包括：Kubernetes CRI shim（测试版）、Docker Compose 支持、用于 AI 代理的 MCP 服务器、策略引擎（Cosign、SBOM、seccomp）、P2P 同步层和 AES-256-GCM 加密机密 — 所有这些都在同一个二进制文件中。

# Apple Silicon VM (macOS default) — 3 s
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun" --attach -t

# Firecracker microVM (Linux) — 400 ms
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --backend vm --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun" --attach -t

# Container (Linux) — 400 ms
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

# Windows WSL2 — same image, same command, same store
pullrun.exe run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

📦 安装

# One command, any platform
curl -fsSL https://github.com/pullrun/pullrun/raw/main/install.sh | bash
平台 你得到什么
macOS brew tap pullrun/tap && brew install pullrun → 原生二进制文件，无 Xcode
Linux APT 包或直接下载、systemd 服务（容器需要 runc，虚拟机需要 /dev/kvm）
视窗 pullrun.exe + WSL2 自动配置、runc + Firecracker
Manual install options 
# macOS (Homebrew — pre-built, no build deps)
brew tap pullrun/tap && brew install pullrun

# From source
make build && export PATH="$PWD/bin:$PATH"

# Cross-compile Windows CLI
cd cli/pullrun && GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o pullrun.exe .

🚀 快速入门

# ── Pull any OCI image ──────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun pull alpine:3.18     # 968 ms — benchmark script at hack/bench.sh

# ── Run as a container (Linux) ─────────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello pullrun"

# ── Run as a microVM (macOS/Linux) ─────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --backend vm --cmd "echo" --cmd "hello" --attach -t

# ── Interactive shell with detach ──────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --tty --attach --cmd /bin/sh
#   Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q  → detach (workload keeps running)
#   pullrun exec <id> -t /bin/sh → re-attach

# ── Background workload ────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --cmd /bin/sleep --cmd 3600

# ── Build & push (no Docker daemon needed) ─────────────────────
pullrun build ./Dockerfile . -t myapp:latest
pullrun build ./Dockerfile . -t myapp:latest --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64
pullrun push <root-digest> ghcr.io/myorg/myimg:latest

# ── Export/import for air-gapped ───────────────────────────────
pullrun save <digest> -o myimage.tar
pullrun load -i myimage.tar

# ── Compose (separate binary) ────────────────────────────────
pullrun-compose up -f myapp/compose.yml               # containers (default on Linux)
pullrun-compose up -f myapp/compose.yml --backend vm   # same compose, VM isolation
pullrun-compose logs -f
pullrun-compose down

# ── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun list                  # all workloads (pending/running/exited)
pullrun stop <id>
pullrun exec <id> /bin/echo hello
# Re-attach to a detached workload (allocates a fresh PTY)
pullrun exec <id> -t -- /bin/sh
# Or, for bidirectional I/O streaming without a new shell:
pullrun workload run <id>

# ── Image management ──────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun images                 # list pulled images in local DAG store
pullrun rmi alpine:3.18       # remove an image, cascade-delete unreachable layers
pullrun rmi sha256:abc...     # also works by digest

# ── GC (DAG store garbage collection) ─────────────────────────
pullrun gc                    # dry-run — report what would be deleted
pullrun gc --apply            # actually delete unreachable nodes
pullrun gc --apply --force    # bypass 90% safety guard

# ── Events & Stats ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun events --types=WORKLOAD_STARTED,POLICY_DENIED
pullrun stats <id>

# ── Network ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun network create my-net --subnet 10.43.1.0/24
pullrun run alpine:3.18 --net bridge

# ── Secrets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun secret create db_password -                     # from stdin
pullrun run myapp:latest --secret db_password

# ── Configs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun config create nginx.conf ./nginx.conf       # from file
pullrun config create nginx.conf -                   # from stdin
pullrun run nginx:latest --config nginx.conf

# ── Diff & Inspect ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun diff <id>
pullrun inspect <id>
pullrun commit <id> myapp:snapshot

# ── Login ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun login ghcr.io
pullrun logout

# ── MCP (AI agents) ────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun mcp                   # stdio mode (for opencode, Claude Code)
pullrun mcp --sse :8080       # SSE mode (for remote agents)

# ── P2P sync ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500

🎯 为什么选择 Pullrun？

🏗️建筑就是一切

Docker 的overlayfs 存储是一个文件系统覆盖——overlayfs 中的CVE（CVE-2023-0386、CVE-2023-32629）可以让容器逃逸到主机。 Pullrun 的内容寻址 DAG 存储按原样存储层，并通过内容哈希进行验证。 没有覆盖，就没有逃脱。

Pullrun 还可以减轻不特定于任何存储驱动程序的共享内核跨容器风险，例如，像 CVE-2026-31431（“复制失败”）这样的内核页面缓存错误会影响共享主机内核的所有容器，无论存储后端如何。 每虚拟机内核隔离（Firecracker、Apple Virtualization）完全包含这些。 DAG 存储使 VM 路径零成本：相同的映像，无需单独的 VM 构建步骤。

Pullrun 默认情况下也是无根的——不需要 sudo，没有监听 TCP 套接字的守护进程，没有来自中央 dockerd 的攻击面。

🔥 唯一具有来自同一映像的容器 + 虚拟机的运行时

后端 隔离 普鲁伦 码头工人 柔和的
Linux 容器（运行） 过程 ✅ ✅ ✅
Firecracker microVM 每个 VM 内核 ✅ ❌ ❌
Apple Silicon 虚拟机 每个 VM 内核 ✅ ❌ ❌
Windows WSL2 容器 过程 ✅ ✅ ❌
Windows Firecracker VM 每个 VM 内核 ✅ ❌ ❌

相同的图像，任何隔离级别。 没有单独的 VM 映像构建步骤。 OCI 清单是 VM rootfs。

🧬 内容寻址 DAG 商店

Pullrun的商店是建立在rkyv + mmap之上的。 OCI 层存储一次，通过内容哈希进行重复数据删除，并直接进行 mmap() 处理 — 无需 tar 提取，无需 overlayfs，无需 dockerd 进程拥有数据。

sha256：摘要是全局一致的。 每个拉取 alpine:3.18 的节点都会在磁盘上存储字节相同的文件。 这使得 P2P 同步变得微不足道：块通过内容哈希进行验证，仅进行增量传输，并在整个集群中进行重复数据删除。

📊 从数字来看

公制 普鲁伦 码头工人
首次高山：3.18拉力 968 ms ~2 s
容器运行延迟 ~400 ms ~800 ms
Apple Virt 虚拟机启动 ~160 ms N/A
Firecracker虚拟机冷启动 ~500 ms N/A
鞭炮虚拟机暖池 ~200 ms N/A
空闲守护进程 RSS 24.6 MiB ~90 MiB
二进制大小（剥离） ~20 MB（CLI + 运行时） ~75 MB
默认无根 ✅ ❌（dockerd 作为 root）
中央守护进程 选修的 必需的

基准：单节点、冷缓存、alpine：Apple M3 (macOS 14) 上的 Pullrun 与 Docker Desktop 4.27 为 3.18。 从运行命令退出到工作负载 PID 生效期间测量的容器运行延迟。 Apple VM 启动测量为交互式执行提示符。 使用 KVM 在 Linux x86_64 上进行 Firecracker 测量；温池配置为 --vm-warm-pool-size 4。 Pullrun 守护进程 RSS 在 pullrun pull alpine:3.18 + pullrun run alpine:3.18 --tty --attach --cmd /bin/sh 之后测量，然后分离。 来自 docker run -d --name idle alpine:3.18 sleep 3600 的 Docker RSS。

以超精细为基准（≥10 次迭代，报告平均值±标准差）。 基准测试脚本位于 hack/bench.sh — 自行运行以重现数字。

✨ 特点

🏃 工作负载生命周期

拉、运行、停止、执行、附加、列表、日志、统计、事件、检查、修剪、rmi、gc plus：

  • commit — 从正在运行的容器的当前文件系统创建一个新的镜像层。 DAG 存储根据原始图像计算增量，生成内容寻址层。
  • diff — 显示正在运行的工作负载与其原始映像之间的文件级更改。 商店比较 DAG 树以生成添加/修改/删除列表。
  • 更新 — 实时资源限制更改（CPU 毫核、内存字节），无需重新启动工作负载。
  • cp — 主机和工作负载路径之间的双向文件复制。
  • 保存/加载 — 适用于气隙环境的 OCI 兼容单文件 tarball 导出/导入。 重新导入会产生相同的内容哈希。
  • 登录/注销 — 私有 OCI 注册表的不记名令牌身份验证。

🔧 Build

本机 Dockerfile 构建器（FROM、RUN、COPY、ADD、WORKDIR、ENV、CMD、ENTRYPOINT），通过指令哈希进行内容寻址层缓存。 直接使用 runc 来运行——没有 Docker 守护进程。 通过 --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 进行多平台构建。

🏗️ OCI 内核镜像

内核二进制文件是一流的 OCI 内容。 pullrun kernel install 将 vmlinux 从 OCI 注册表下载到 DAG 存储中。 --kernel-image 标志允许任何工作负载通过 OCI 参考指定自定义内核 - 相同的存储支持、内容寻址管道：通过摘要验证、永久缓存、可推送到任何注册表。

🐳 撰写

完整的 Docker Compose 兼容工作流程：up、down、logs、ps、build。 支持依赖性排序（拓扑排序）、端口映射、环境变量、卷（绑定安装）、资源限制（CPU/内存）、标签和用于隔离的每个项目桥接网络。 通过 compose-spec/compose-go 库解析标准 docker-compose.yml 文件。

每个服务都可以作为容器或虚拟机运行 - 使用 --backend vm 从同一个组合文件将所有服务作为 Firecracker microVM 启动，无需进行任何更改。

☸️ Kubernetes CRI

位于 cri/pullrun-cri/ 的嵌入式 CRI shim — 从 Kubernetes CRI API 实现 RuntimeService 和 ImageService。 将 pod 沙箱映射到 pullrun 工作负载，支持 RuntimeClass (pullrun-container / pullrun-vm)、用于映像/CPU/内存覆盖的 pod 注释以及流式传输（exec、attach、端口转发）。

本机控制平面存根位于 control-plane/: pullrun-controller（调度程序）+ pullrun-agent（每个节点部署程序）中，通过 gRPC 进行通信。 这是一个正在进行的 v1 工作，计划使用 etcd、.pullrun.local DNS 和准入控制 — 但有线协议目前很稳定。

🤖 MCP 人工智能集成

本机模型上下文协议服务器将 15 个运行时操作公开为 MCP 工具 — 运行、停止、执行、列表、获取、检查、日志、统计、pull_image、list_images、构建、推送、修剪、compose_up、compose_down 以及 MCP 资源（pullrun://workload/{id}、pullrun://workload/{id}/logs、pullrun://store/info、pullrun://images）。 在 stdio 模式（适用于 opencode、Claude Code、Cursor）或 SSE 模式（适用于通过 HTTP 的远程代理）下工作。

🔐 策略引擎

在工作负载运行之前对其进行控制 - 内置支持：

  • Cosign签名验证（Ed25519密钥对，密钥ID匹配）
  • SBOM评估（CVSS评分，许可证禁止）
  • Seccomp 配置文件（约 50 个系统调用的默认允许列表、无限制或自定义 JSON）
  • 只读 rootfs — 防止运行时篡改（只写入 --volume 挂载）
  • no_new_privileges — 阻止 setuid 和 capset 升级
  • 策略是声明性的：required_signature：true，max_cvss_score：7.0，deny_licenses：[“GPL-3.0”]

组成所有四个： --require-signature --readonly-rootfs --no-new-privileges --seccomp-profile 默认值。

🌐 P2P 图片分发

节点通过 gRPC + Bloom 过滤器点对点共享图像块。 一个节点从注册表中提取数据，其余节点相互进行增量同步。 功能：用于零配置 LAN 对等点查找的 mDNS/发现、用于避免冗余传输的布隆过滤器缓存、用于对等点状态的八卦协议、增量计算、用于对等点跟踪的注册服务。

📡 网络

用户定义的桥接网络，具有 IPAM、入站/出站端口转发、DNS 解析和 iptables 集成。 每个工作负载通过 --net 有四种模式：隔离（容器默认，仅在 10.42.0.1 上使用主机代理环回）、桥接（共享 pullrun-br0 桥接、工作负载间通信）、slirp（虚拟机默认，通过 slirp4netns 进行用户空间 NAT — 无桥接、无 iptables）、主机（共享主机命名空间）、无（无网络）。 隔离是由代理而不是按工作负载 VLAN 强制执行的。

🗝️ 加密的秘密

静态 AES-256-GCM 加密，运行时解密到工作负载 tmpfs 中。 pullrun Secret create/get/ls/inspect/rm — 数据在磁盘上保持加密状态，只有运行时进程才能解密。

🔄 导出/导入

适用于气隙环境的单文件 OCI 兼容 tarball 导出。 重新导入会产生相同的内容哈希。

📊 事件和可观察性

通过 pullrun 事件的实时事件流 — IMAGE_PULLED、WORKLOAD_STARTED、POLICY_DENIED 等。每个工作负载的 CPU/内存统计信息。 守护进程中内置的 Prometheus 指标导出器。 部署/中的 PrometheusRule 警报配置。

🧹 DAG 存储垃圾收集和引用计数 rmi

pullrun rmi 通过标签或摘要删除图像，并立即级联删除不可达的子树节点。 每个节点的引用计数（node.refcount sidecar 文件）保留共享层——在删除最后一个引用图像之前，另一个图像引用的层永远不会被删除。 守护进程启动时通过 recompute_all_refcounts 进行崩溃恢复，从所有标记的映像和工作负载根重建引用计数。

pullrun gc 回收无法从任何标记的映像或正在运行的工作负载访问的无法访问的 DAG 节点（孤立层、清单、blob）。 功能：默认空运行（仅报告）、--应用于实际删除、--强制绕过 90% 安全防护、运行中操作的 op-lock 保护、通过 kernel_image_digest 固定 VM 内核映像。 商店不再永远增长。

🖥️ 交互式 Shell

完整的 TTY 支持，可通过 Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q 分离/重新连接。 适用于所有后端（容器、Firecracker VM、Apple VM）。 分离的工作负载继续运行 - 使用 pullrun exec --tty <id> /bin/sh 重新附加。 甚至可以处理已退出的工作负载（守护进程启动一个新的睡眠容器）。

❤️ 健康检查和重启政策

pullrun run --health-cmd 'curl -f http://localhost:80' — 定期进行健康检查，并可配置间隔、超时和重试。 --restart on-failure|always|unless-stopped 控制退出时自动重新启动。 健康状态通过 pullrun 检查和 pullrun 事件显示。

🗄️ VM 状态持久化

停止的虚拟机的行为就像休眠的机器一样 - 所有写入都会保留，并根据需要使用 exec 重新启动。 后端细节：Apple Virt rootfs 通过 VirtioFS 持续存在，Firecracker 保留 ext4 映像，Container (runc) rootfs 是短暂的（仅 --volume 安装存活）。 无需重新拉动。

🔐 私人注册表

pullrun login <registry> 用于不记名令牌身份验证。 守护进程接受 --insecure-registry 用于纯 HTTP 镜像和气隙 LAN 缓存。

☸ 库伯内斯

Pullrun 在 cri/pullrun-cri/ 中提供了一个 CRI shim，它实现了 Kubernetes 容器运行时接口。 它将 pod 沙箱映射到 pullrun 工作负载并支持：

  • RuntimeClass — pullrun-container 用于 runc 容器，pullrun-vm 用于 Firecracker VM
  • Pod 注释：pullrun.io/image、pullrun.io/cpu-millicores、pullrun.io/memory-bytes
  • 流式传输：执行、附加、端口转发
  • 通过 DAG 存储进行图像管理

部署为 DaemonSet，清单位于部署/中：

kubectl apply -f deploy/runtime-daemon.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/serviceaccount.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/servicemonitor.yaml

🤖 MCP 人工智能集成

任何兼容 MCP 的 AI 代理（opencode、Claude Code、Cursor）都可以通过自然语言控制 pullrun：

# Start the MCP server
pullrun mcp

# In opencode or Claude Code, the agent can now:
#   "pull alpine and run it as a VM"
#   "exec into my-app and check the logs"
#   "show me all running workloads"
#   "run docker-compose up from my project"

MCP 服务器公开 15 种工具和 4 种资源类型。 远程代理的 SSE 模式：pullrun mcp --sse :8080。

🌐 P2P 图片分发

# Node A — seed
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9500

# Node B — pulls delta blocks from Node A
pullrun-runtime daemon --sync-addr 0.0.0.0:9501 \
  --sync-peers node-a.example.com:9500

每个块在接受之前都会通过内容哈希进行验证 - 不需要信任。

🏗️建筑

                              pullrun (Go CLI)
                   pull · run · build · compose · inspect
                   events · stats · network · secret · mcp
                              │
                              │ gRPC (UDS or TCP)
                              ▼
                   ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
                   │         pullrun-runtime             │
                    │  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────────────┐ │
                    │  │ Store    │   │ Executor         │ │
                    │  │ (DAG)    │   │ runc / VM        │ │
                    │  │ └ rkyv   │   │ └ VmPool (warm)  │ │
                    │  │ └ mmap   │   │                  │ │
                    │  │ └ DashMap│   │                  │ │
                   │  └────┬─────┘   └──────┬───────┘    │
                   │       │                │            │
                   │  ┌────┴────────────────┴────┐       │
                   │  │       Network            │       │
                   │  │  IPAM · Proxy · DNS      │       │
                   │  └──────────────────────────┘       │
                   │                                      │
                   │  Sync · Policy · Secrets · Metrics   │
                   └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                   ┌──────────┴──────────┐
                   │      Kubernetes     │
                   │  CRI shim · Runtime │
                   │  Class · Prometheus │
                   └─────────────────────┘

存储使用 DashMap<Digest, Arc<Mmap>> 进行无锁并发读取 - 第一个读取器为 mmap() + 页面错误付费，每个后续读取器都是单个原子加载。 Rust 工作区有 11 个包（store、oci、exec、vm、net、dns、vsock、sync、policy、runtime、init）； Go 方面是 CLI、CRI shim、compose 和控制平面存根。

📁 项目布局

proto/            # Protobuf definitions (single source of truth)
runtime/          # Rust workspace — core data plane
  pullrun-store/   # Zero-copy DAG store (rkyv + mmap + DashMap concurrent cache)
  pullrun-oci/     # OCI client + DAG converter
  pullrun-exec/    # Executor trait + runc wrapper
  pullrun-vm/      # Firecracker + Apple Virt backends, warm VM pool (pool.rs), pullrun-init guest agent
  pullrun-net/     # IPAM, proxy, DNS, iptables, slirp4netns
  pullrun-dns/     # In-process DNS server for workload resolution
  pullrun-vsock/   # Vsock transport layer for VM guest-host communication
  pullrun-sync/    # P2P block sync (Bloom, mDNS, gossip)
  pullrun-policy/  # Cosign, SBOM, seccomp gates
  pullrun-runtime/ # gRPC daemon
cli/pullrun/      # Go CLI (cobra) — 30+ commands
cri/pullrun-cri/  # Kubernetes CRI shim
control-plane/    # Multi-node orchestration stub: pullrun-controller + pullrun-agent (v1 WIP)
cmd/pullrun-compose/ # Docker Compose-compatible CLI
deploy/           # K8s manifests, Prometheus rules, alerts
docs/             # Architecture, operations, policy, MCP
tools/            # Standalone smoke-test workspaces (apple-virt-exec, firecracker-smoke, etc.)
